column6: Static hold to release tight quads/psoas before loading.
Strengthens shin muscles prevents knee pain. Lean on an upright with heels forward.
Loads quad while stretching psoas of rear leg. Knee over toe is safe here.
Upright torso. Core heavy. Alternate: hack squat on the Sliding Bench with shoulder pads for the same quad bias without the rack work.
column7: Squat inside the rack with safety spotters set.

column6: Heavy quad volume with zero spinal load. Full foot on the plate; do not let the knees cave in.
column7: Do not lock the knees hard at the top.

column6: Recline the torso back to lengthen the rectus femoris. Squeeze at top — but the stretched bottom half drives the most growth. Last set: bottom-half partials after failure.
Extreme quad stretch. Use support if needed — hold an upright of the rack.
Inner thigh work replacing the adductor machine. Crucial for pelvic stability and often weak in men.
Toes on the step under the padded Smith bar. Targets Gastrocnemius (upper calf). Heavy weight.
Anti-extension: squeeze glutes; ribs down; neutral neck.
Rotation: rotate from the trunk; let the hips follow.
Metabolic finisher: drive the knees; keep hips low and stable.
